text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development by ensuring quality and reliability. Unit tests check individual components of code, such as functions or methods, to verify they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, examine how different parts of a system interact, catching issues that may arise when combining modules. One popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where developers write tests before writing the actual code. This process encourages simpler, more reliable designs from the start. Ultimately, thorough testing gives developers confidence that changes or new features won’t break existing functionality. This confidence allows teams to deliver software faster and with fewer bugs.
